conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances. Let me start by gathering some basic information:

Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child - my son Alex. He's 17 years old and I'd like to find out if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I can help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me ask you: Is Alex currently in approved education or training (such as A-levels, Scottish Highers, or an approved training course)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he is! Alex is currently in sixth form studying for his A-levels. He's doing really well actually - taking Maths, Physics and Chemistry.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great to hear that Alex is doing well in his studies!

Now I need to ask: Do you currently live with Alex in the UK, and are you responsible for him (meaning you're paying for things like his food, clothes, and housing)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - Alex lives at home with me. We're in Birmingham. And yes, I'm responsible for all his expenses - food, clothes, school supplies, everything really. He's a typical teenager so there's always something he needs!

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information! Let me just gather a few more details to complete the assessment.

Are you or your partner currently receiving Child Benefit for Alex from another country (outside the UK)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, we're not receiving any Child Benefit from another country - it's just us here in Birmingham, no international payments or anything like that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. Just one more question to complete my assessment:

Is there anyone else who might also be claiming Child Benefit for Alex, such as another parent or guardian?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him. It's just me looking after Alex - I'm his only parent involved in his care.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for answering all my questions. Based on the information you've provided, I can now assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why: Even though Alex is 17 (over the usual age limit of 16), you still qualify for Child Benefit because he's under 20 and is in approved education - his A-level studies in sixth form count as approved education. Since you live with Alex in the UK, you're responsible for all his expenses, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him, all the eligibility requirements are met.

**Important note:** Child Benefit will continue as long as Alex remains in approved education or training and is under 20 years old. If he leaves education, there are specific rules about when Child Benefit stops, so make sure to inform HMRC if his circumstances change.

Also, be aware that if you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge. You can still claim Child Benefit, but you might have to pay some or all of it back through tax.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about claiming Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 17 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 17 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old and currently studying A-levels in sixth form, which qualifies as approved education. Under UK Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit for children under 16, BUT you'll still qualify if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. Alex meets this criterion as he is 17 and in approved education (A-levels). Additionally, you live with Alex in the UK (Birmingham), you are responsible for him (paying for his food, clothes, school supplies, and all expenses), nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him, and you are not receiving Child Benefit from another country. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 17-year-old son Alex. Alex is in approved education (studying A-levels in sixth form), lives with the claimant in the UK, the claimant is responsible for all his expenses, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. All eligibility requirements are satisfied.
